title: Database Schema
description:
This spec previously described the Ladybug-specific physical schema for the code graph. That backend-specific schema now belongs to `code-graph:code-graph/ladybug-graph-store`, while storage-agnostic behavior belongs to `code-graph:code-graph/graph-store`.

This spec remains only as a transition marker so existing references can be removed deliberately rather than silently losing context.
context:
This spec previously described the Ladybug-specific physical schema for the code graph. That backend-specific schema now belongs to `code-graph:code-graph/ladybug-graph-store`, while storage-agnostic behavior belongs to `code-graph:code-graph/graph-store`.

This spec remains only as a transition marker so existing references can be removed deliberately rather than silently losing context.

rules:
Ladybug-specific schema requirements SHALL be defined by `code-graph:code-graph/ladybug-graph-store`, not by this standalone spec.

Storage-agnostic code, specs, and commands MUST depend on `code-graph:code-graph/graph-store` rather than this spec.

This spec introduces no new runtime requirements of its own and exists only to support the migration away from a backend-specific standalone schema spec.

constraints: This spec is deprecated and scheduled for removal once remaining references are updated
New storage-agnostic specs MUST NOT depend on this spec
Ladybug-specific schema requirements belong in code-graph:code-graph/ladybug-graph-store

scenarios.name: Existing readers are redirected to replacement specs
when: a contributor needs the Ladybug-specific physical schema requirements
then: AND storage-agnostic behavior is read from code-graph:code-graph/graph-store

scenarios.name: Deprecated spec adds no new runtime behavior
when: this deprecated spec remains present during the migration
then: it serves only as a transition marker
AND it does not introduce additional runtime requirements beyond the replacement specs
